entries.title: Trio knows Horcrux quest from start of Year 7

content.fact: At the start of Year 7, Harry, Ron, and Hermione are not attending a normal Hogwarts year; they are preparing for and conducting the Horcrux hunt.
content.injection: In canon Year 7 Context, write the trio as Horcrux-hunt participants rather than ordinary students. They may know the mission, but their information is still incomplete and should update at each anchor.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 7 Horcrux quest active; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import later-book knowledge, deaths, relationships, or reveals into this Context.

entries.title: Hallows not active before Lovegood explanation

content.fact: Before Xenophilius explains the symbol and tale, the Deathly Hallows are not an active interpretive framework for the trio.
content.injection: Before the Lovegood explanation, do not have Harry, Ron, or Hermione reason fluently about the Elder Wand, Resurrection Stone, or Hallows quest; they may have objects/clues without understanding the system.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.

entries.title: Locket destroyed only after Forest of Dean

content.fact: After the Ministry infiltration, the trio possesses the locket Horcrux, but it remains active and corruptive until Ron destroys it with Gryffindor’s sword.
content.injection: Before the locket-destroyed anchor, keep the locket as an active burden and Horcrux influence; do not treat it as destroyed or psychologically neutral early.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.

entries.title: Harry-as-Horcrux truth hidden until Snape memories

context.label: Before Harry learns he must die

content.fact: Before Snape’s memories, Harry, Ron, and Hermione do not know that Harry himself carries a piece of Voldemort’s soul and must walk to death.
content.injection: Before the Harry-Horcrux truth anchor, aggressively block characters from saying Harry is a Horcrux or planning around his self-sacrifice unless accepted story lore exposed it early.
constraints: This is a major endgame spoiler gate and should outrank broad Horcrux cards.
After the truth anchor, Harry may understand the sacrifice but Ron and Hermione still do not automatically know unless he tells them.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Harry learns he must die; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not let Hermione deduce Harry-as-Horcrux early without an explicit story branch.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Voldemort defeated only after final duel

content.fact: Before the final duel, Voldemort remains alive and dangerous; the remaining Horcrux problem is not resolved.
content.injection: Before Voldemort’s defeat anchor, do not write him as dead, politically gone, or postwar history. After the anchor, switch to aftermath and avoid keeping active-war Voldemort in standard canon.

entries.title: Kreacher locket truth hidden until story

content.fact: After finding the R.A.B. clue, the trio do not yet know Kreacher role or the full Regulus locket story until Kreacher tells it.
content.injection: Before Kreacher explains the locket, allow Kreacher to know his own history, but block the trio and narration from treating Regulus, the cave, or Mundungus locket chain as already known.
constraints: The initials can prompt investigation without granting the full backstory.
Kreacher may withhold or reveal depending on the scene.
Use this as a reveal-control gate: allow the named keepers to know the secret, but block characters outside that knowledge path from stating, acting on, or narrating it as known.

content.fact: The Bathilda encounter is a trap using Nagini; Harry and Hermione should not know that before the attack reveals it.
content.injection: Before the Godric Hollow trap springs, allow Voldemort side to know the trap, but block Harry, Hermione, and narration from identifying Bathilda as Nagini or treating the visit as knowingly compromised.
constraints: Unease and wrongness are allowed; confirmed trap knowledge is not.
Do not let later knowledge make Harry avoid the scene without an AU clue path.

entries.title: Voldemort name Taboo hidden until capture

content.fact: The name Taboo is a hostile tracking secret; Death Eaters can use it, but the trio should not know the full mechanism until the capture path exposes it.
content.injection: Before the Snatcher capture reveals the Taboo, allow Ron to fear the name and Death Eaters to exploit the system, but block the trio and narration from explaining the full tracking rule as known.
constraints: Avoid making the trio perfectly operationally aware too early.
The secret controls knowledge of the tracking mechanism, not whether danger exists.
Use this as a reveal-control gate: allow the named keepers to know the secret, but block characters outside that knowledge path from stating, acting on, or narrating it as known.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Taboo capture reveals tracking; if the scene is outside this window, withhold this secret gate.

entries.title: Narcissa lie hidden during forest aftermath

context.label: Before Narcissa lie reaches the defenders

content.fact: Narcissa privately lies about Harry death; Voldemort and his followers should not know Harry is alive until the battle resumes.
content.injection: During the forest aftermath, allow Harry and Narcissa to know the lie, but block Voldemort, Death Eaters, Hogwarts defenders, and narration outside Harry perspective from treating Harry survival as public knowledge.
constraints: This is a narrow battlefield secret with a short reveal window.
Do not let the defenders or Voldemort react to Harry survival before it is exposed.

entries.title: Elder Wand allegiance hidden until final duel

content.injection: Before the final duel, allow Harry to reason toward the wand allegiance only after the relevant clues, but block Voldemort, Death Eaters, and neutral narration from knowing the allegiance answer early.
constraints: Voldemort may pursue a mistaken theory without knowing the real allegiance chain.
Do not let the model shortcut the wand mystery before the final confrontation.
